content: That message indicates that the profile has been tampered with or moved from another PC improperly. If you are willing to start over, click to c:\users\<yourid>\appdata\roaming and DELETE the folder named thunderbird. Do this with thunderbird not running. Then restart thunderbird and it should create a baseline profile. If you had transferred from another computer, we can assist, probably, in getting this fixed. Tell us more.
creator: davidsk

I can't advise you from personal experience - I haven't yet received the "invitation" to migrate from Comcast, and I'm happy to put it off as long as possible - but I can point you to the two articles, one from Comcast, the other from Yahoo, that supposedly describe the process. Have you read them carefully?

content:
I think there are some outdated instructions floating around out there. Try this:

In the Address Book, click the little icon to the right of the New Contact button (see attached image.) Give the new list a name. You are supposed to be able to start typing addresses from your Address book in there, but I find it doesn't always work. What works more reliably is to confirm the Mailing list, then start drag-and-dropping entries from the Address Book onto the new Mailing List. See the attached animation.
creator: rednoise
